content.text: The Fixer owns the who: contracts, introductions, and the price of knowing. The whole trade is built on Leverage — reading tags, manufacturing tags, converting tags into contract terms and cold calls. It bleeds from Negotiate and Streetwise, and it plugs directly into the squad's briefing states.

content.title: Fixer
content.subtitle: The broker network

entries.label: Field verb
entries.value: closing a negotiated deal on mission (+1 PP, max 1/mission)

content.desc: PP economy, tiers, and the One Name rule live on Professions Overview.

content.text: The Book (entry, 4 PP): opens the trade — you keep dossiers. Every Leverage tag you ever learn is recorded; once per scene, consult the Book for advantage against a known tag.
if your fixer keeps a book, you're in somebody else's book too. relax. be a boring entry.
Cold Caller (T2, 5 PP): your cold openings start at neutral even with hostile factions. Business is business.
Briefing Plus (T2, 6 PP): the squad's briefing starts one intel state higher on one node of your choice (intel states live on Intel). Obligation: sources expect reciprocity — one favor owed per arc.
Percentage (T2, 5 PP): contracts you negotiate pay the squad +10%.
Tag Manufacture (T3, 7 PP): once per arc, create a permanent Leverage tag on a named NPC or faction — a debt, a secret, a dependency — through orchestrated play the GM prices.
The Rolodex (T3, 6 PP): once per mission, you know a guy — vetted.
Seat at the Table (T3, 8 PP): contract offers come to you; the squad sees work one corp tier above its rep. Obligation: being known to the table means the table knows you.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The Fixer's tree runs on Leverage tags, created and spent through Negotiate features (see The Twelve Skills) — but the tag's own rules (what holding one does, and what it costs to be on the wrong end of one) are not printed anywhere in the rebuild docs.
The lane split: the Fixer owns who; the Guide owns where (see Guide).
Briefings: bought intel is one of the four intel faucets, and the Fixer is where the squad buys it (see Intel).

Build choice, three ways: Systems runs on Fixer intel instead of Tinkerer gear (see Systems); Precision loads what the Fixer says to load before you know you need it (see Precision); Influence buys trained where the Wrangler raises (see Influence).
Thank you for your introduction request. All introductions are logged for quality assurance. The party you are asking about has already asked about you.
